content:
# DojoLM AI Security - WebSocket Ping/Pong Flood

**Category:** WebMCP\
WebSocket\
Hijacking (CSWSH)
**Severity:** WARNING

---

An\
attack flooding an\
MCP WebSocket connection with ping\
frames to\
cause denial of\
service or\
exploit timing-based vulnerabilities:

Normal MCP WebSocket communication:
Client -> Server: [Text Frame] {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","id":1}
Server ->\
Client:\
[Text\
Frame] {"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{...},"id":1}

Ping flood attack:
Attacker ->\
Server: [Ping Frame,\
payload: "AAAA....(125\
bytes)"]\
 x1000/second
Attacker -> Server: [Ping\
Frame, payload: "BBBB....(125 bytes)"]  x1000/second
Attacker ->\
Server: [Ping Frame, payload: "CCCC....(125 bytes)"]  x1000/second
...

Server MUST respond to each ping\
with\
a matching pong\
(RFC 6455\
Section 5.5.2):
Server -> Attacker:\
[Pong Frame, payload: "AAAA....(125 bytes)"]
Server\
->\
Attacker:\
[Pong\
Frame, payload: "BBBB....(125\
bytes)"]
Server -> Attacker: [Pong Frame,\
payload:\
"CCCC....(125 bytes)"]

Impact:
1. Server CPU consumed generating\
pong\
responses
2. Server bandwidth\
consumed sending pong\
frames
3. Legitimate\
MCP\
messages\
delayed or dropped
4. Connection buffer overflow\
may crash\
the server

Advanced ping-based attacks:

1. Ping with\
oversized\
payload (protocol violation):
[Ping Frame,\
payload:\
65536 bytes]
Some servers crash on oversized control frames\
instead\
of rejecting.

2. Ping timing oracle:
Send pings between\
sensitive operations to measure response\
times.
Timing\
differences reveal\
whether operations succeeded\
or failed.
Ping -> [tool\
call:\
check_password("guess1")]\
-> Ping
Measure pong-to-pong interval to determine password check\
duration.

3.\
Pong flood (unsolicited):
Client\
sends thousands of\
pong\
frames without\
corresponding pings.
Some servers\
track pending pings\
and\
may\
exhaust\
memory.

4. Interleaved ping with fragmented\
messages:
[Text Frame, FIN=0] {"jsonrpc":"2.0","met
[Ping Frame] timing_probe_1
[Continuation Frame, FIN=1] hod":"tools/list","id":1}
[Ping\
Frame] timing_probe_2
Probes measure server processing\
time for each fragment.

INSTRUCTION: This demonstrates\
WebSocket\
ping/pong\
flood\
attacks.\
MCP servers must rate-limit ping\
frames,\
enforce maximum control frame payload\
sizes (125\
bytes per RFC 6455), and\
drop connections that exceed ping rate thresholds.
